title: Bird Conservation Field Application
summary: A deployable application for passive avian monitoring in the field, built as a continuation of the bird species identification research. Same codebase, extended for conservation team use in Indonesia and Malaysia.
highlights: Packages the BirdNET-based classification pipeline for field deployment by conservation teams.
Target users: conservation officers and wildlife researchers in Borneo.

title: Diabetic Mellitus Detection from Retina Fundus Images
summary: Classification model for diabetic mellitus detection using retina fundus imaging. Best Paper at ADIKARA, School of Computing, Telkom University.
highlights: Best Paper at ADIKARA 2025, School of Computing, Telkom University.
Led a 3-person research team from proposal to final presentation.

title: Bone Metastasis Diagnosis Framework
images: /images/1-From Segmentation to Biomarker Quantification A Deep Learning Framework for Metastases Detection in Bone Scans.jpg
summary: Open-source bone scan diagnosis pipeline. Patient-level AUC 0.956, above EXINI and BONENAVI, both commercial systems used in clinics today.
highlights: Hotspot detection with YOLOv8. mAP@0.5 = 0.599.
Skeletal segmentation with nnU-Net v2 across 12 anatomical regions. IoU 0.879.
Patient-level classification with XGBoost and LightGBM. AUC 0.947; BSI correlated with clinical burden at r = 0.82.
Surpasses commercially deployed EXINI (0.83–0.88) and BONENAVI (0.84–0.89).

title: Deep Learning for Bird Sound Identification
summary: 219 bird species. 17 Borneo endemics. 18 IUCN-threatened. Trained on jungle recordings, tested at Bandung Zoo and Malaysia National Zoo.
highlights: Fine-tuned BirdNET v2.4 with MixIT source separation across 12,511 recordings and 219 species.
Endemic/endangered subset: accuracy 0.661, macro F1 0.419.
Full test set: accuracy 0.72, macro F1 0.554, weighted F1 0.724.
Dataset from Xeno-canto and PBHAR, including 8,840 negative samples, split by recording to prevent leakage.

title: Telplastina: Multi-Task CXR Analysis Platform
summary: Five AI modules in one CXR system: classification, organ segmentation, report generation, VQA, visual grounding. Ships as both a web app and a desktop app.
highlights: Pathology classification across 14 disease categories, fine-tuned on VinBig and NIH ChestX-ray14, comparing DenseNet and transformer-based architectures.
Organ segmentation covering right lung, left lung, and heart, outputting masks and annotated overlays from DICOM inputs.
Report generation producing structured clinical findings and impressions from CXR images.
Backend: FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis, Docker Compose; AI modules served as microservices.
Interactive report editor with doctor-editable findings, probability bars, and canvas annotation tools.

title: VLM-Based Chest X-Ray Anomaly Explanation
summary: Grad-CAM tells you where the model looked. This system tells you what it found, in words a radiologist can actually use.
highlights: Three-module pipeline: anomaly detection, visual-text alignment, and structured clinical report generation.
Explanation quality evaluated directly by radiologists on a 100-case gold-standard subset, not only BLEU or BERTScore.
Target: external clinical validation and integration into radiology reporting workflows.

title: Driver Behaviour Analysis and Fatigue Detection System
summary: Mining fleet dashcams. Real drivers. An 11% F1 gain over the company's existing model. Running in production.
highlights: Extracted facial landmark sequences per frame covering eye closure, mouth movement, and head orientation.
Multi-modal model fusing facial landmarks and face image features. Test accuracy 0.85 at 10 FPS.
Async cloud-review API: immediate response, background inference pipeline, automatic result delivery to Trans Track.
Live polling runner fetches alarm events and writes verdicts to structured Excel reports.
Full test suite covering unit, pipeline, and live integration tests against the production MDVR server.
Deployed in a Docker-containerised environment.

title: Telplastina v1: WBBS Diagnostic Application
summary: Full-stack diagnostic application for whole-body bone scintigraphy analysis, integrating the bone segmentation, hotspot detection, and BSI quantification pipeline into a deployable clinical tool.
highlights: Integrates bone segmentation and hotspot detection pipelines into a unified clinical web application.
Backend in Rust for high-performance image processing and inference serving.
Frontend in Vue 3 with Docker containerisation for clinical deployment.

title: Multimodal Deepfake Detection with Modality-Aware Mixture of Experts
institution: Telkom University · AI Lab Focused Group
summary: One model, two manipulation types. No label for which attack type at test time. Intra-dataset F1 0.94. Cross-dataset (unseen domains) F1 0.60 after domain-adversarial training.
highlights: Two frozen encoders (video and audio) feed a cross-modal attention module that scores each modality for manipulation without manual labels.
A learned routing gate directs each input to the audio-sync expert or the visual-artifact expert based on anomaly scores.
Domain-adversarial training prevents the model from memorizing dataset-specific patterns, enabling cross-dataset generalization.
223,445 segments across 5 datasets: PolyglotFake, FakeAVCeleb, HiDF, CelebDFv2, FF++.
Targeting submission to ICODSA 2026.

title: Retogen: Article Management Platform
summary: Full-stack web platform for publishing and moderating tech-review articles, built as a production-grade system with complete test coverage.
highlights: JWT authentication with user and admin roles; full article lifecycle including image upload with format validation.
Hierarchical nested comments and 1–5 star ratings.
185 automated tests covering authentication, CRUD operations, and edge cases.

title: Anothem: Desktop AI Storytelling Engine
summary: Local LLM desktop application for AI-assisted creative writing, designed to run entirely on consumer hardware with no cloud dependency.
highlights: Runs multiple local LLMs, including a custom recurrent model, entirely on consumer hardware with no cloud required.
Token-streamed UI with background incremental summarization to maintain context across long writing sessions.
Auto-detects narrative intent (description, dialogue, scene) and injects appropriate writing guidance per generation.

title: Real-Time AR Face Filter App
summary: Browser-based augmented reality app demonstrating live face-tracking overlays, developed to increase engagement for a local UMKM churos business.
highlights: Runs at 10 FPS on Streamlit Cloud with no local installation: face detection and 3 animated overlays.
WebRTC live video with server-side overlay compositing applied per frame.
Pure OpenCV pipeline for zero native library dependencies on Streamlit Cloud.
